heading: Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem restoration
label: 1962d–22 Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem restoration
notes: References in TextThe Great Lakes Basin Compact, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is not classified to the Code.
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 2000, and not as part of the Water Resources Planning Act which comprises this chapter.

children.text: the Great Lakes comprise a nationally and internationally significant fishery and ecosystem;
the Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem should be developed and enhanced in a coordinated manner; and
the Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem provides a diversity of opportunities, experiences, and beneficial uses.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “Great Lake” means Lake Superior, Lake Michigan, Lake Huron (including Lake St. Clair), Lake Erie, and Lake Ontario (including the St. Lawrence River to the 45th parallel of latitude).

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “Great Lake” includes any connecting channel, historically connected tributary, and basin of a lake specified in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Great Lakes Commission
children.label: 2 Great Lakes Commission
children.text: The term “Great Lakes Commission” means the Great Lakes Commission established by the Great Lakes Basin Compact ().82 Stat. 414

children.heading: Great Lakes Fishery Commission
children.label: 3 Great Lakes Fishery Commission
children.text: The term “Great Lakes Fishery Commission” has the meaning given the term “Commission” in .section 931 of title 16

children.heading: Great Lakes State
children.label: 4 Great Lakes State
children.text: The term “Great Lakes State” means each of the States of Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Minnesota, Ohio, Pennsylvania, New York, and Wisconsin.

children.heading: Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem restoration
children.label: c Great Lakes fishery and ecosystem restoration

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall develop a plan for activities of the Corps of Engineers that support the management of Great Lakes fisheries.December 11, 2000

children.heading: Use of existing documents
children.label: B Use of existing documents
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the plan shall make use of and incorporate documents that relate to the Great Lakes and are in existence on , such as lakewide management plans and remedial action plans.December 11, 2000

children.label: i
children.text: the signatories to the Joint Strategic Plan for Management of the Great Lakes Fisheries; and

children.label: ii
children.text: other affected interests.

children.label: A
children.text: to identify methods of restoring the fishery, ecosystem, and beneficial uses of the Great Lakes; and

children.label: B
children.text: to determine whether planning of a project under paragraph (3) should proceed.

children.heading: Projects
children.label: 3 Projects
children.text: The Secretary shall plan, design, and construct projects to support the restoration of the fishery, ecosystem, and beneficial uses of the Great Lakes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a program to evaluate the success of the projects carried out under paragraph (3) in meeting fishery and ecosystem restoration goals.

children.heading: Studies
children.label: B Studies
children.text: Evaluations under subparagraph (A) shall be conducted in consultation with the Great Lakes Fishery Commission and appropriate Federal, State, and local agencies.

children.heading: Recreation features
children.label: 5 Recreation features
children.text: A project carried out pursuant to this subsection may include compatible recreation features as determined by the Secretary, except that the Federal costs of such features may not exceed 10 percent of the Federal ecosystem restoration costs of the project.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: d Cooperative agreements
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary may enter into a cooperative agreement with the Great Lakes Commission or any other agency established to facilitate active State participation in management of the Great Lakes.

children.heading: Relationship to other Great Lakes activities
children.label: e Relationship to other Great Lakes activities
children.text: No activity under this section shall affect the date of completion of any other activity relating to the Great Lakes that is authorized under other law.

children.heading: Development of plan
children.label: 1 Development of plan
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of development of the plan under subsection (c)(1) shall be 65 percent.

children.heading: Project planning, design, construction, and evaluation
children.label: 2 Project planning, design, construction, and evaluation
children.text: Except for reconnaissance studies, the Federal share of the cost of planning, design, construction, and evaluation of a project under paragraph (3) or (4) of subsection (c) shall be 65 percent.

children.heading: Credit for land, easements, and rights-of-way
children.label: A Credit for land, easements, and rights-of-way
children.text: The Secretary shall credit the non-Federal interest for the value of any land, easement, right-of-way, dredged material disposal area, or relocation provided for carrying out a project under subsection (c)(3).

children.heading: Form
children.label: B Form
children.text: The non-Federal interest may provide up to 100 percent of the non-Federal share required under paragraphs (1) and (2) in the form of services, materials, supplies, or other in-kind contributions.

children.heading: Operation and maintenance
children.label: 4 Operation and maintenance
children.text: The operation, maintenance, repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of projects carried out under this section shall be a non-Federal responsibility.

children.heading: Non-Federal interests
children.label: 5 Non-Federal interests
children.text: In accordance with , for any project carried out under this section, a non-Federal interest may include a private interest and a nonprofit entity.section 1962d–5b of this title
